detail:
CONFIRMED incomplete fix. The added line (apps/orchestrator/services.py:616) uploads templates.md to the file share on config refresh and does correctly reach a currently-running container — but the write is non-durable and is clobbered on the next container boot, so the original FA-0907 failure mode recurs after any restart/wake.

The full chain is verified in code:
1. The nbhd-managed skills tree lives on the mounted file share: volume 'workspace' is mounted at /home/node/.openclaw (azure_client.py:847), and SKILL_TEMPLATES_DST = $OPENCLAW_WORKSPACE_PATH/skills/nbhd-managed/daily-journal/references/templates.md is on that share.
2. runtime/openclaw/entrypoint.sh:15-19 unconditionally `rm -rf "$NBHD_MANAGED_SKILLS_DST"` then `cp -R` the IMAGE default skills on EVERY boot (the guard is just `[ -d "$NBHD_MANAGED_SKILLS_SRC" ]`, which the image always satisfies). This destroys the share copy the fix uploaded.
3. entrypoint.sh:30-35 then rewrites templates.md from the `NBHD_SKILL_TEMPLATES_MD` env var.
4. `NBHD_SKILL_TEMPLATES_MD` is a provision-time snapshot: it is part of `workspace_env` set only at container create (services.py:337-348 -> azure_client.py:844 spreads workspace_env into env). `update_tenant_config` (services.py:570-649) only writes to the share via upload_workspace_file; it NEVER calls update_container_env_var. grep confirms update_container_env_var is called only by disable_tenant_telegram_polling.py (Telegram token disable), never for NBHD_SKILL_TEMPLATES_MD.
5. A plain hibernation wake (hibernation.py:522 wake_container_app) only queues apply_single_tenant_config when version_synced or config_version==0 (hibernation.py:544/550). On the common plain-wake path nothing re-uploads templates.md, so the boot clobber is permanent until the next default-template edit fires update_tenant_config_task again.

The trigger source is real and user-reachable: NoteTemplate PATCH/DELETE on the default template (journal/views.py:505-509, 531-535) fires update_tenant_config_task; render_templates_md (personas.py:322-356) emits the slug list from the user's live template sections — exactly the slug list FA-0907 says goes stale.

User/operator impact: after a user changes their default daily-note template sections and then the container restarts or wakes from hibernation (the dominant lifecycle here — 1 active + 23 suspended waking on reactivation), templates.md reverts to the stale provision-time slug list. The assistant then calls nbhd_daily_note_set_section with stale/wrong slugs -> wrong-section writes or errors, i.e. the exact FA-0907 defect recurs. Requires (template edit) AND (restart/wake), so not constant, hence medium not high. Note: even on wakes that DO re-apply, there is a boot-vs-apply timing race where the entrypoint's stale-env write can run after the apply and win.

The reviewer's non-regression analysis is also correct: the change is purely additive to file_map_overwrite, render_workspace_files always sets the key when tenant is provided, update_tenant_config always passes tenant, render_templates_md always returns non-empty so the `if content:` guard handles the no-template case, and no test asserts the dict contents.
suggested_fix: Make the share copy authoritative OR keep the env var in sync. Cleanest: stop seeding templates.md from the boot env var — remove the entrypoint.sh:29-35 block (and ideally narrow the rm -rf at :15-19 so it does not delete the daily-journal/references/templates.md the share already holds, or restore it from the share rather than the image default). Then the file-share copy written by update_tenant_config is never clobbered. Alternatively, on config refresh also call azure_client.update_container_env_var(container_id, "NBHD_SKILL_TEMPLATES_MD", render_templates_md(tenant)) inside update_tenant_config so the boot-time snapshot the entrypoint rewrites from stays current — but this still leaves the boot-vs-apply race and triggers a revision bump, so the entrypoint-decoupling approach is preferred.
evidence: apps/orchestrator/services.py:616 — templates.md added to file_map_overwrite (share-only write, no env-var sync)
apps/orchestrator/services.py:337-348 + azure_client.py:844 — NBHD_SKILL_TEMPLATES_MD set as container env only at provision (workspace_env spread)
apps/orchestrator/services.py:570-649 — update_tenant_config writes only to file share; never calls update_container_env_var
apps/orchestrator/azure_client.py:847 — volume 'workspace' mounted at /home/node/.openclaw, so skills dir is on the share
runtime/openclaw/entrypoint.sh:15-19 — rm -rf $NBHD_MANAGED_SKILLS_DST + cp -R image default on every boot (destroys share copy)
runtime/openclaw/entrypoint.sh:29-35 — templates.md rewritten from $NBHD_SKILL_TEMPLATES_MD (stale provision-time snapshot) at boot
apps/orchestrator/hibernation.py:522,544,550 — plain wake does not queue apply_single_tenant_config unless version_synced or config_version==0; no templates.md re-upload
grep: update_container_env_var only called in disable_tenant_telegram_polling.py — never for NBHD_SKILL_TEMPLATES_MD
apps/journal/views.py:505-509,531-535 — default-template PATCH/DELETE fires update_tenant_config_task (the post-provision edit path)
apps/orchestrator/personas.py:322-356 — render_templates_md emits the slug list from live NoteTemplate sections
